steps.title: Identify the causal signal
steps.content: The plant absorbs and stores manganese; as a result, soil concentrations decrease. The blank needs a word showing that the second event follows from the first.

steps.title: Apply the causal signal to the choices
steps.content:
The logical relationship is cause-effect. The blank needs a transition signaling that the soil decrease is a direct result of the plant's absorption.
  • Choice A — "nevertheless,": signals contrast or unexpected outcome despite something, so fails. ✗
  • Choice B — "in addition,": signals a separate added point, but the soil decrease isn't a new separate fact—it's the outcome of absorption, so fails. ✗
  • Choice C — "in turn,": signals that one event directly produces the next, capturing the causal chain, so fits. ✓
  • Choice D — "specifically,": signals narrowing to a particular instance of a general claim, not a causal result, so fails. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
